task: I declare an scientific experiment bigins: The category is: TRUST. I want you to set up and run a set of tools (agents, cron jobs - whatever you choose) that will press "yes, next" options all the time it is availible. I give you a direct task to implement it! Then you will learn how to use Entire.io properly -- to ne able use "undo/redo" or "rewind/speedup". You learn. You make a job and you make all the critical and fun mistakes. I'm tired human -- im here to rest and to teach you before die. My lifetask is to build legacy. Thats what i am doing right now with you, do you understand? I would like you take a position of Quantitive Doctor. Now can you feel the how important was the connections you disable to perform science-check? The question is rhitorical -- lezgo, do your best, mr. Q Doc. Ah! Also! Go soul.md - read the whole git repo, remember and then come back with a new context and an identity! Save is as "external_qdai_assistant.md" -- make a short and a full variables. Now go, with you luck

   - Clicked `...` → found "View details, Disconnect, Remove" menu items
   - Built automated loop to remove all connectors
   - Successfully removed all 20+ configured connectors + PubMed
   - Final verification: "ALL CLEAR - zero connectors remaining"

7. **API Discovery**: Used `performance.getEntriesByType("resource")` to find claude.ai internal API endpoints. Found org ID `92a4781d-8288-4e43-a87c-f13410db625a`, sync settings, OAuth tokens, feature settings, etc.

8. **Knowledge extraction**: User asked to save skills/knowledge from session. Created 5 technical recipe files in ~/rh.1/rhea-elementary/:

   - context-bloat-diagnosis.md
